---
title: "Zachary Thomas McDowell"
image: "portrait-small.jpg"
links:
  - label: '<i class="fab fa-github fa-lg"></i> GitHub'
    url: "https://github.com/ztm76617"
  - label: '<i class="fab fa-linkedin fa-lg"></i> LinkedIn'
    url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/zach-mcdowell34/"
  - label: '<i class="fas fa-envelope fa-lg"></i> Email'
    url: "mailto:zmcdowe7@uw.edu"
output:
  postcards::trestles
site: distill::distill_website
---
<!-- The postcard had a useless second scrollbar (in addition to the big one of distill). For some reason, I couldn't remove it with the CSS in custom.css. My only solution is to modify the source of the postcards::trestle template to remove this scrollbar. -->
<!-- Same for font family -->
```{r, include = FALSE}
trestles_orig <- readLines(
  system.file("pandoc_templates/trestles.html",
              package = "postcards")
)

trestles_mod <- trestles_orig

trestles_mod[
  which(
    grepl(
      'class="d-flex flex-column col px-5 align-items-center align-self-center mh-100" style="overflow-y: scroll;"',
      trestles_mod
    )
  )
] <- '<div class="d-flex flex-column col px-5 align-items-center align-self-center mh-100" style="overflow-y: none;">'

trestles_mod <- gsub("'Roboto Slab'", '-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Oxygen, Ubuntu, Cantarell, "Fira Sans", "Droid Sans", "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif', trestles_mod)

trestles_mod <- gsub('style="height:35rem"', 'style="width: 80%; max-width: 360px"', trestles_mod)

cat(paste(trestles_mod, collapse = "\n"), file = system.file("pandoc_templates/trestles.html",
              package = "postcards"))

```

<br>

I am a doctoral student in Political Science at the University of Washington. My research sits at the intersection of comparative and international political economy, with a focus on income inequality, redistribution, and the politics of economic crisis across advanced democracies.

I use time-series econometrics and cross-national data to study how shifts in capital investment and profit rates shape welfare state outcomes. Prior to UW, I completed my BA and MA at the University of Georgia.

<br>


```{css, echo = FALSE}
.columnname {
  font-size: 1.1rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #102542;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.07em;
  border-bottom: 2px solid #dae7f1;
  padding-bottom: 0.3rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.8rem;
  display: block;
}
.grad-item {
  list-style: none;
  background-image: url(graduation-cap-solid.png);
  background-repeat: no-repeat;
  background-size: 1.2rem;
  background-position: 0 0.2rem;
  padding-left: 1.8rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.65rem;
}
.grad-degree {
  font-size: 1.05rem;
  color: #1d3549;
  font-weight: 500;
}
.grad-years {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: rgba(0,0,0,0.5);
}
.grad-location {
  font-size: 0.9rem;
  color: #4682b4;
  font-style: italic;
  margin: 0;
}
```

:::: {style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 40% 52%; grid-column-gap: 30px; line-height: 1.6rem; margin-top: 0.5rem;"}

::: {}
<span class="columnname">Interests</span>

* Time-Series Econometrics
* International & Comparative Political Economy
* Politics of Income Inequality & Redistribution
* Causes and Consequences of Economic Crises
* Cross-National Trends in Capital Investment
* R Programming
:::

::: {}
<span class="columnname">Education</span>

<ul style="padding-left:0; margin-top:0.4rem;">
<li class="grad-item">
  <span class="grad-degree">Ph.D. Student, Political Science</span><br>
  <span class="grad-years">2024 &ndash; Present</span><br>
  <p class="grad-location">University of Washington</p>
</li>
<li class="grad-item">
  <span class="grad-degree">M.A., Political Science</span><br>
  <span class="grad-years">2020 &ndash; 2022 &nbsp;&middot;&nbsp; Minor in Quantitative Research Methods</span><br>
  <p class="grad-location">University of Georgia</p>
</li>
<li class="grad-item">
  <span class="grad-degree">B.A., Political Science</span><br>
  <span class="grad-years">2017 &ndash; 2019</span><br>
  <p class="grad-location">University of Georgia</p>
</li>
</ul>
:::
::::
